lean_stdlib_notes.nat_add_mod: Nat.add_mod (a b n : Nat) : (a + b) % n = (a % n + b % n) % n
lean_stdlib_notes.no_split_ifs: split_ifs is a Mathlib tactic, NOT available in stdlib. Use by_cases instead.
lean_stdlib_notes.by_cases_usage: by_cases h : P <;> simp [h] works well for if-then-else goals
lean_stdlib_notes.strong_induction: Use 'induction n using Nat.strongRecOn with | ind n ih => ...' for strong induction
lean_stdlib_notes.no_push_neg: push_neg is Mathlib only. Use omega or Nat.lt_of_not_le instead.
lean_stdlib_notes.eq_1_lemma: For recursive functions, use 'rw [f.eq_1]' to unfold one step without simp loops.
lean_stdlib_notes.no_nat_decideGt_eq: Nat.decideGt_eq does NOT exist in Lean 4.28/4.29 stdlib
lean_stdlib_notes.no_list_get?: List.get? does NOT exist in Lean 4.29! Use l[i]? (List.getElem? notation)
lean_stdlib_notes.getElem?_eq_none: List.getElem?_eq_none : l.length ≤ i → l[i]? = none (direct function, NOT an Iff - do not use .mpr)
lean_stdlib_notes.list_any_eq_true: List.any_eq_true : l.any p = true ↔ ∃ x ∈ l, p x = true — available in Lean 4.28 stdlib
lean_stdlib_notes.by_contra_not_available: by_contra may fail in some contexts. Use 'rcases Nat.lt_or_ge a b with h | h' for case splits instead.
lean_stdlib_notes.no_le_rfl: le_rfl does not exist in stdlib. Use Nat.le_refl _ instead.
lean_stdlib_notes.filter_cons_form: List.filter_cons uses 'if p x = true then ...' not 'if p x then ...'. After 'cases h : s v', hv may be unused since s v is substituted.
lean_stdlib_notes.committedIndex_safety_needs_hn: committedIndex_safety (voters : List Nat) (acked : AckedFn) (hn : 0 < voters.length) : majority ... ≤ countGe ... — requires explicit non-empty proof
lean_stdlib_notes.calc_chain_for_nat_le: Use calc chains with ≤ and = steps (mixing is fine in Lean 4) to avoid omega/linarith issues
lean_stdlib_notes.overlapCount_eq_filter_length: overlapCount voters s = (voters.filter s).length. Proof: induction; cons case: 'cases h : s v <;> simp [ih] <;> omega'
lean_stdlib_notes.SC1_bridge: SC1_overlapCount_eq_countGe: overlapCount voters (fun v => decide (acked v >= k)) = countGe voters acked k. Used for cross-module quorum reasoning.
